rationale: Wuyts et al. (2004) is cited in the theory section when the authors argue that PICs involve more than dyadic relationships and therefore require extradyadic analysis. The citation is also revisited in the discussion to position the study as part of the move beyond dyadic channel perspectives. Although the citing paper does not directly reuse Wuyts et al.'s method, it relies on that work to ground its core framing of triadic channel governance, making the role more than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Wuyts et al. (2004) as prior evidence that channel phenomena should be analyzed beyond dyads, which supports its triadic/extradyadic framing of partially integrated channels. This prior work helps justify the paper's conceptual setup for including cross-member feedback and brand-related extradyadic effects in governance decisions.
